title: L-theanine sleep evidence context
review_notes: Uses a systematic review of standalone L-theanine trials rather than caffeine-combination studies.
Avoids an insomnia-treatment claim and preserves uncertainty about clinical populations and objective outcomes.
No evidence tier, dosage, safety, governance, or publishing-control fields are changed.

sources.title: Examining the effect of L-theanine on sleep: a systematic review of dietary supplementation trials

changes.new_value: A 2025 systematic review identified 13 human trials of standalone L-theanine for sleep-related outcomes and reported possible benefits across subjective and objective measures. Trial designs, doses, and populations varied, and better studies in people with clinical insomnia are still needed; this supports cautious sleep-support language, not an insomnia-treatment claim.
changes.rationale: Adds a current synthesis of standalone L-theanine evidence while explicitly limiting the claim to sleep support.
